scope: Artifact-only operator approval packet for the IF09-FIND-001 S3 remediation plan.
remediation_plan_options: Approve a future local remediation only if canonical sync packet assembly derives latest_completed_project_commit_sha from verified primary refs before any packet write or promotion.
Approve a future local remediation only if SHA mismatch handling is fail-closed or emits an explicit drift artifact instead of silently normalizing packet lineage.
Approve a future local remediation only if regression coverage is anchored to IF10-REG-001 at tests/test_active_context_canonical_sha_sync.py.
Reject any option that expands beyond active_context_governance hardening into runtime, product, Bedrock, secrets, or real_apply scope.
approval_criteria: The remediation plan must stay bounded to canonical SHA verification hardening for IF09-FIND-001 and must not require runtime or product-surface changes.
The plan must specify an objective pass condition for verified-primary-ref selection before canonical packet write.
The plan must specify objective mismatch handling: fail closed or emit an explicit drift artifact linked to the evidence chain.
The plan must require regression proof for tests/test_active_context_canonical_sha_sync.py covering canonical SHA equality, mismatch handling, and preservation of forbidden surfaces as false.
The plan must require before/after evidence proving stale project SHA metadata no longer persists silently in canonical packet lineage.
The plan must preserve remediation_apply_authorized=false, patch_authorized=false, real_execution_authorized=false, finding_close_authorized=false, and revalidation_authorized=false at this gate.
rejection_or_block_criteria: Reject if the plan requires applying a patch, executing remediation, or opening runtime in this gate.
Reject if the candidate change surface is too ambiguous to identify the canonical sync packet assembly and primary-ref verification boundaries.
Reject if mismatch handling still permits silent SHA normalization or untracked custody drift.
Reject if regression evidence for IF10-REG-001 cannot be defined concretely at tests/test_active_context_canonical_sha_sync.py.
Reject if the plan depends on structural schema drift in ACTIVE_CONTEXT_STATE.json or ACTIVE_CONTEXT_SCHEMA.json outside approved scope.
Reject if any step would invert real execution, remediation apply, patch apply, finding-close, secrets, product, Bedrock, or real_apply locks.
future_local_remediation_minimum_require: A future remediation gate must identify the exact canonical sync control responsible for selecting and persisting latest_completed_project_commit_sha.
A future remediation gate must document a deterministic verified-primary-ref selection rule before any canonical packet materialization.
A future remediation gate must define how drift evidence is emitted or how fail-closed behavior triggers when recorded SHA and verified SHA diverge.
A future remediation gate must materialize regression coverage for IF10-REG-001 without opening runtime or external network scope.
A future remediation gate must preserve all forbidden execution surfaces as false throughout planning, implementation, and proof.
future_local_remediation_required_eviden: A remediation implementation decision artifact tied to IF10-REM-001 and IF10-RCA-001.
Pass evidence for tests/test_active_context_canonical_sha_sync.py with the required assertions from IF10-REG-001.
A before/after comparison showing the stale-SHA condition from IF09-FIND-001 and the corrected canonical packet behavior.
A drift artifact or fail-closed sample demonstrating that mismatch evidence is surfaced explicitly and not normalized away.
Validator pass evidence and a no-real-execution / no-real-apply attestation for the remediation proof gate.
An evidence-link update tying the remediation proof back to IF09-FIND-001, IF10-REM-001, IF10-REG-001, and IF10-REVAL-001.
future_rollback_requirements: A future remediation gate must define a rollback path that restores the prior canonical sync control if regression coverage fails.
Rollback evidence must preserve mismatch custody and must not erase drift artifacts already emitted.
Rollback must keep validator pass, or the remediation gate must abort before any state promotion claim is made.
future_blast_radius_limits: Blast radius is limited to active_context_governance and canonical sync packet verification surfaces.
No runtime mutation, product behavior, Bedrock readiness, secrets, network, MCP, RAG, memory-write, or real_apply surfaces may be touched by the future local remediation.
No unrelated findings, candidate findings, or invalid findings may be promoted or modified by this remediation track.
